title: Margin trading hits ₹1 trillion. The hidden risks in your stock investment plan
summary: Leveraged stock buying is booming in India, but margin calls, interest costs, and haircuts can quickly magnify losses for retail investors

title: ₹82 lakh crore mutual fund boom signals shift from FDs – but are households over-equitising?
summary: Monthly SIP inflows stood at ₹29,845 crore in February 2026, after crossing ₹31,000 crore in January. Total mutual fund assets are now about ₹82 lakh crore, with SIP assets alone at roughly ₹16.36 lakh crore. Nearly 9.92 crore SIP accounts are actively contributing.

title: Middle East crisis: Top mutual fund categories to protect your investments
summary: Investors should stay conservative and look at categories such as short-duration funds, corporate bond funds with high-rated portfolios, banking and PSU debt funds, and gilt funds. These categories can add fixed returns and regular payouts while keeping credit risk lower.

title: March madness: Why the market’s panic misleads investors
summary: A World Cup win, a war, oil at $119, drones over refineries, a bank chairman’s exit—and the month isn’t even over. The market panic still belongs to borrowed-money investors, not you.

title: Income Tax Rules 2026: Will there be changes in tax slabs from 1 April 2026? All FAQs answered
summary: New Income Tax Rules, 2026: With the New Income Tax Rules 2026 being published by the government, buzz about a changed income tax slabs is in the air. Read on to find out if there will be any changes in the income tax slabs from 1 April, 2026.

title: Income tax refund still pending? Here's why it may be delayed and what happens next
summary: The deadline for filing income tax returns for assessment year 2025-26 was extended to September 15, 2025. Many taxpayers are still waiting for refunds. Here's why it may be delayed and what happens next.

title: Iran war: How to rebalance your portfolio and manage risk in volatile markets
summary: Before making any changes to their portfolio, investors should evaluate their financial goals, risk appetite, return expectations, and current asset allocation. Any restructuring should be considered only after a thorough review of these factors.

title: Middle-East crisis: Why G-Secs and AAA bonds may shield your portfolio in uncertain times
summary: In uncertain environments, investors tend to move up the credit curve rather than down it. This means preference shifts towards issuers such as public sector undertakings, large financial institutions, and well capitalised corporates.

title: RBI moves to prevent banks from mis-selling financial products: How will it protect customers?
summary: The RBI directions to banks include obtaining the customer’s explicit consent, ensuring product suitability for the customer before it is marketed, and giving a refund with compensation where mis-selling is established.

title: Middle East conflict unlikely to hurt Indian economy's structural strength, says Radhika Rao of DBS Bank
summary: Rao said escalating tensions in the Middle East and resultant risks from pipeline inflationary pressure indicated that the RBI's monetary policy committee would prefer to keep the repo rate unchanged.

title: Foreign investors dump Rs 88,000 crore in March; 2026 outflows cross Rs 1 lakh crore
summary: Foreign investors have pulled out Rs 88,180 crore (about USD 9.6 billion) from Indian equities so far this month, weighed down by escalating tensions in West Asia, a weakening rupee and concerns over the impact of elevated crude oil prices on India's growth and corporate earnings.

title: UTI AMC CEO says safety margin in smallcaps limited, backs financials and manufacturing
summary: UTI Asset Management Company CEO Vetri Subramaniam warns of limited margin of safety in small caps despite corrections. He favours financials, manufacturing and capital goods, urging disciplined investing, diversification, SIPs, and long-term focus amid volatility and evolving market conditions.

title: Mcap of five of top-10 most valued firms erodes by Rs 1 lakh cr; HDFC Bank biggest laggard
summary: The combined market valuation of five of the top-10 most-valued firms eroded by Rs 1 lakh crore last week, with HDFC Bank taking the biggest hit.

title: From Turbulence to Stability: Pat Dorsey’s timeless guide to picking winning stocks
summary: Amid global volatility, moat investing—popularised by Warren Buffett and Pat Dorsey—focuses on companies with durable advantages. Key moat sources include intangible assets, switching costs, network effects, and cost leadership, helping firms sustain profits and reduce long-term investment risk.

title: ​10 Sensex stocks with up to 60% upside potential - Are these in your portfolio?​
summary: 10 Sensex stocks stand out with projected gains of 30% to 60%, signaling solid return prospects in a market that’s becoming increasingly selective.

title: Concentration risk in PMS & curated portfolios: Lessons ahead of Sebi’s 2026 overhaul
summary: India's Portfolio Management Services (PMS) sector is booming, reaching ₹41.59 lakh crore. While concentration can drive returns, SEBI's 2026 rule changes aim to enhance governance and investor protection. The article highlights the hidden risks of layered concentration in both PMS and curated portf

title: IPO rush next week: 7 public offers worth over Rs 2,000 crore to open for subscription
summary: Three mainboard IPOs and four SME offerings open this week, aiming to raise over Rs 1,900 crore. Powerica leads the pack, with all issues open March 24–27 amid cautious investor sentiment and volatile markets.

title: 9 penny stocks surge up to 430% in 3 months; 2 turn multibaggers. Do you own any?​
summary: Nine penny stocks have delivered impressive gains ranging from 10% to 430%, including three multibaggers. These outperformers were identified using specific filters: a market capitalisation below Rs 1,000 crore, a share price under Rs 20, and a minimum trading volume of 5 lakh shares.​

summary: Markets ended the week marginally lower, with the Nifty breaching key support levels and slipping below moving averages. Rising volatility and oversold RSI indicate continued weakness.
